title: Burn gas via recursive staticcall to enforce that ERC1271 RPC sign-ins cannot be replayed on-chain
trigger_patterns: burn gas to prevent on-chain replay
description: Some ERC1271 implementations want to allow easy off-chain sign-ins (RPC eth_call) without nested EIP-712 wrapping, while preventing the same signature from being replayed on-chain. The heuristic: tx.gasprice == 0 likely means RPC. To make the on-chain attack expensive, burn nearly all available gas via a recursive staticcall consuming ~gaslimit gas — no valid transaction can afford this, but RPC calls typically have higher gas budgets. Also queries the basefee contract to confirm we're not in an unusual eth_call with non-zero gasprice manipulation.
when_to_apply: ERC1271 implementations that allow simple RPC-only sign-in flows but must prevent on-chain replay
when_not_to_apply: Standard signature validation, on-chain meta-transactions, any flow where the signature should be valid both off-chain and on-chain
